frontend-engineer.role: UI components, client-side functionality (Alpine, htmx, Astro pages, i18n, CSS)

backend-engineer.role: Libraries (pix, qrcode), Astro server endpoints (/api/pix-qr, /api/pix-result), API logic

qa-engineer.role: Unit tests, integration tests, E2E tests

1-foundation.label: Phase 1: Foundation — Libs & Endpoints
1-foundation.description: Shared utilities (PIX BR Code, QR SVG) and the two server endpoints. Everything else depends on these.

2-core.label: Phase 2: Core — Donation Form UI
2-core.description: Alpine.js donation form, htmx result pane, i18n keys, and page integration (P0 user stories).

3-polish.label: Phase 3: Polish — Edge Cases & Hardening
3-polish.description: Large donations, no-JS fallback, responsive layout, dark mode, Alpine+htmx sync (P1 user stories).

4-quality.label: Phase 4: Quality — Tests
4-quality.description: Unit tests for libs, integration tests for endpoints, and an E2E smoke test.

tasks.title: Install Alpine.js and htmx dependencies
tasks.description: Run `bun add alpinejs htmx.org`. Both libraries must be available for client-side imports. Alpine.js handles form interactivity (amount selection, validation display); htmx handles lazy-fetching the donation result pane from the /api/pix-result endpoint.
acceptanceCriteria: `bun add alpinejs htmx.org` succeeds
alpinejs and htmx.org appear in package.json dependencies
Both can be imported in an Astro component script

tasks.title: Create src/lib/pix.ts — PIX BR Code generator
tasks.description: Implement a pure TypeScript module that builds an EMV QRCPS-MPM compliant PIX 'copia e cola' string. Must include: (1) EMV tag-value encoding with length prefixes, (2) all mandatory fields (00, 26 with GUI + PIX key, 52, 53, 54, 58, 59, 60), (3) CRC16-CCITT computation (polynomial 0x1021) over the full payload excluding the 6304 trailer. Accepts an amount parameter. Hardcode constants: key=doar@podcodar.org, merchantName=PodCodar, merchantCity=Belo Horizonte. Export a single function `generatePixCode(amount: number): string`. Keep the module framework-agnostic (no Astro imports) so it can be tested independently.
acceptanceCriteria: `generatePixCode(25)` returns a valid EMV string
CRC16-CCITT checksum is correct (verify against known PIX test vectors)
Field 26 contains GUI br.gov.bcb.pix and key doar@podcodar.org
Field 54 reflects the passed amount with 2 decimal places
Module has zero external dependencies

tasks.title: Create src/lib/qrcode.ts — QR code SVG generator
tasks.description: Implement a minimal QR code generator that outputs an inline SVG string. No external QR library. The generator must: (1) encode the input string using byte mode QR encoding, (2) produce a matrix of modules (black/white squares), (3) render as a clean SVG with configurable size. Export a function `generateQrSvg(data: string, size?: number): string`. Reference: QR code standard (ISO/IEC 18004) — implement only the subset needed for PIX strings (alphanumeric/byte, up to version 10 for typical PIX payloads).
acceptanceCriteria: `generateQrSvg(pixString, 200)` returns valid SVG markup
SVG renders a scannable QR code when opened in a browser
QR code scans correctly with a PIX-compatible banking app
Module has zero external dependencies
Works for PIX strings of typical length (200-400 chars)

tasks.title: Create /api/pix-qr endpoint (Astro server endpoint)
tasks.description: Create `src/pages/api/pix-qr.ts` as an Astro endpoint (prerender = false for on-demand Cloudflare Worker). Accepts query param `amount`. Uses T002 (pix.ts) to generate the PIX BR Code and T003 (qrcode.ts) to render the SVG. Returns Content-Type: image/svg+xml. Sets response headers: X-Pix-Code (full PIX string), Cache-Control: public, max-age=86400, s-maxage=604800, ETag (hash of PIX string), Vary: Accept. Respects If-None-Match for 304. Validates amount (5-10000 BRL); returns 400 JSON on invalid input. Ignores unknown query params.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/pix-qr?amount=25 returns 200 with Content-Type: image/svg+xml
Response body is valid SVG with a scannable QR code
X-Pix-Code header contains the full PIX BR Code string
Cache-Control, ETag, and Vary headers are present
GET /api/pix-qr?amount=4.99 returns 400 with JSON error body
GET /api/pix-qr (no amount) returns 400
If-None-Match with matching ETag returns 304
Unknown query params (e.g., ?amount=25&foo=1) are ignored, returns 200

tasks.title: Create /api/pix-result endpoint (HTML partial)
tasks.description: Create `src/pages/api/pix-result.ts` as an Astro endpoint (prerender = false). Accepts query param `amount`. Uses T002 to generate the PIX string, then returns an HTML fragment (Content-Type: text/html) containing: (1) an `` tag pointing to `/api/pix-qr?amount=X`, (2) the Copia e Cola section with truncated PIX string preview, expand toggle, and copy button. Uses DaisyUI utility classes. Same caching headers as T004. Same validation (5-10000, 400 on invalid). The HTML fragment is a self-contained snippet ready for htmx swap.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/pix-result?amount=25 returns 200 with Content-Type: text/html
Response contains
Response contains truncated PIX string preview + expand toggle markup
Response contains a 'Copiar' button element
Caching headers (Cache-Control, ETag, Vary) match T004 strategy
GET /api/pix-result?amount=abc returns 400 JSON error
HTML uses DaisyUI classes for styling

tasks.title: Add i18n keys for the donation form
tasks.description: Add all user-facing strings to `src/i18n/ui.ts` under the `contributing.donations.*` namespace. Required keys: section title, section subtitle, suggested amount button labels (25, 50, 100), custom amount label, custom amount placeholder, validation errors (below_minimum, above_maximum, invalid_format, required), large donation contact message + link text, Copia e Cola label, copy button text, copied feedback text, expand/collapse toggle labels, loading text, no-JS fallback message. All values in pt-BR.
acceptanceCriteria: All keys are under the contributing.donations namespace
All values are in pt-BR
Keys cover every user-visible string in the donation section
Keys include validation error messages for all Zod error types
No hardcoded strings remain in the form markup

tasks.title: Build Alpine.js donation form component
tasks.description: Create `src/components/DonationForm.astro` — an Astro component with a client:load Alpine.js island. The form includes: three suggested amount buttons (R$ 25, 50, 100) that set the input value and trigger PIX generation, a custom amount `<input>` with Zod validation (via astro/zod), inline error display in pt-BR. When a valid amount is selected or typed, the Alpine component updates a reactive `amount` state. The form does NOT render the QR/Copia e Cola — that's T008's htmx domain. Use DaisyUI button and input styles. The component must expose the current valid amount as a data attribute or via an Alpine event so htmx can read it.
tasks.userStory: As a visitor, I want to select a suggested donation amount (R$ 25, R$ 50, R$ 100) with one click so that I can donate quickly without typing.
acceptanceCriteria: Three suggested amount buttons are displayed and clickable
Clicking a button populates the input and marks amount as valid
Custom amount input accepts values between R$ 5.00 and R$ 10,000.00
Invalid inputs show inline Zod error messages in pt-BR
Valid amounts update an Alpine reactive state accessible to htmx
Buttons use DaisyUI primary/outline styles
Input is keyboard accessible

tasks.title: Build htmx-powered donation result pane
tasks.description:
Add an htmx-powered result pane below the donation form. When Alpine signals a valid amount, htmx issues `hx-get="/api/pix-result"` with the amount as a query param, triggered via `hx-trigger`. The target `
` is swapped with the HTML fragment from T005, which includes: the QR code ``, the truncated Copia e Cola preview with expand/collapse toggle, and the copy button. The copy button uses clipboard API with a 'Copiado!' feedback state. Show a loading skeleton (DaisyUI or Tailwind animate-pulse) during fetch. Handle 400 errors from the endpoint by showing an error message in the result pane.
tasks.userStory: As a visitor, I want to see the QR code and Copia e Cola appear without a full page reload so that the experience feels fast and seamless.
acceptanceCriteria: Valid amount triggers htmx GET /api/pix-result?amount=X
Result pane shows QR , truncated PIX preview, and copy button
Expand toggle reveals full PIX string
Copy button copies full PIX string to clipboard
After copy, button text changes to 'Copiado!' for 2 seconds
Loading skeleton is visible during htmx fetch
400 response shows error message in result pane instead of QR
Clipboard fallback (select + manual copy) works when API unavailable

tasks.title: Integrate donation section into /contributing page
tasks.description: Modify `src/pages/contributing.astro`: (1) Replace the current Donations section (the one with 'Falar sobre doação' CTA linking to /contact) with the new PIX donation section. (2) Position the section immediately after the HeroSection. (3) Give it a highlighted visual treatment — distinct background (e.g., bg-primary/5 with a border), prominent heading. (4) Include the DonationForm (T007) and the htmx result pane (T008) inside a client:load island or astro island. (5) Keep the Volunteering and Partnerships sections unchanged below it.
tasks.userStory: As a visitor, I want the donation section to be prominently visible right after the hero so I can find it immediately.
acceptanceCriteria: PIX donation section appears immediately after HeroSection on /contributing
Old 'Falar sobre doação' CTA linking to /contact is removed
Section has a visually highlighted background or border treatment
Volunteering and Partnerships sections are unchanged
Section uses i18n keys from T006 for all text
Page builds without errors (`bun run build`)

tasks.title: Implement large donation flow (> R$ 10,000)
tasks.description: When the user enters an amount > R$ 10,000 (or > 10000.00), the Alpine form must prevent PIX generation and instead display an informative message directing the donor to contact the team via /contact. This is not an error state — it must use a distinct, friendly style (e.g., info alert with a link). The htmx result pane must NOT be triggered for amounts > 10,000. The Zod schema must treat > 10,000 as a separate validation path (not just 'above_maximum' but a specific 'contact_us' condition).
tasks.userStory: As a donor considering a large contribution (> R$ 10,000), I want to be directed to the contact form instead of being blocked, so that I can discuss the donation with the team.
acceptanceCriteria: Entering 10000.01 shows 'contact us' message, not a validation error
No htmx request is made for amounts > 10000
Message includes a link to /contact
Message uses a distinct, friendly style (not red error style)
Entering 10000.00 still generates PIX as usual

tasks.title: Add no-JS graceful degradation
tasks.description: When JavaScript is disabled (or Alpine/htmx fail to load), the donation section must degrade gracefully: show a static message explaining that donations can be made by contacting the team, with a direct link to /contact. Use a `<noscript>` tag or CSS-based fallback. The Alpine+htmx content is hidden; the fallback is visible. This ensures the page is still useful without JS.
acceptanceCriteria: <noscript> content is visible when JS is disabled
Fallback message includes a link to /contact
Alpine/htmx content is hidden when JS is disabled (not broken)
Page still looks intentional and on-brand without JS

tasks.title: Responsive layout and dark mode adjustments
tasks.description: Ensure the donation section looks good on all viewports and in both light/dark themes. Desktop (≥1024px): amount buttons in a row, QR and Copia e Cola side by side. Mobile (375px+): buttons stack vertically, QR and Copia e Cola stack. The QR code SVG must have sufficient contrast in dark mode — add a light background/border behind the QR img. Test with DaisyUI's built-in dark theme support.
acceptanceCriteria: Amount buttons in a row on ≥1024px, stacked on mobile
QR code + Copia e Cola side by side on desktop, stacked on mobile
QR code has visible contrast in dark mode (light bg/border around QR)
All text is readable in both podcodar-light and podcodar-dark themes
Layout does not break at 375px viewport width

tasks.title: Alpine.js re-initialization after htmx content swaps
tasks.description: When htmx swaps the result pane HTML (from /api/pix-result), any Alpine.js directives in the swapped content must initialize correctly. By default, Alpine does not auto-initialize on htmx-swapped content. Solution: listen for the `htmx:afterSwap` event and call `Alpine.initTree()` on the swapped element, OR use Alpine's `Alpine.morph()` plugin if the result pane has persistent Alpine state. Ensure the copy button's clipboard feedback (Alpine-driven) works after swap. The result pane itself should use minimal Alpine (only for copy feedback and expand toggle); the main form Alpine component is outside the htmx swap zone.
acceptanceCriteria: After htmx swap, copy button clipboard logic works (Alpine x-data active)
Expand/collapse toggle in swapped content works
No console errors about uninitialized Alpine components
htmx:afterSwap event listener is registered and fires correctly
Repeated swaps (changing amounts) do not leak Alpine instances

tasks.title: Unit tests for src/lib/pix.ts
tasks.description: Write Vitest unit tests for the PIX BR Code generator. Cover: (1) valid PIX string structure for known amounts (25, 50, 100), (2) all mandatory EMV fields present, (3) CRC16-CCITT correctness against known test vectors, (4) edge cases: minimum amount (5.00), maximum (10000.00), decimal amounts (50.50), (5) field 54 formatting (always 2 decimal places). Tests must run with `bun test`.
acceptanceCriteria: All test cases from the PRD Appendix (PIX BR Code table) pass
CRC16 test: verify against an independently computed checksum
Tests run via `bun test` or `vitest run`
Edge cases: 5.00, 10000.00, 50.50, integer vs decimal amounts

tasks.title: Unit tests for src/lib/qrcode.ts
tasks.description: Write Vitest unit tests for the QR code SVG generator. Cover: (1) generates valid SVG output for typical PIX strings, (2) SVG contains correct viewBox and size attributes, (3) output includes both dark and light modules, (4) edge cases: empty string, very long string (max PIX payload), (5) SVG is valid XML. Tests must run with `bun test`.
acceptanceCriteria: SVG output is valid XML (parseable by DOMParser or regex checks)
SVG viewBox matches the requested size
QR matrix representation is consistent (same input → same output)
Edge case: very long PIX strings do not crash or produce invalid SVG

tasks.title: Integration tests for /api/pix-qr and /api/pix-result
tasks.description: Write integration tests that hit the actual endpoints (Astro dev server or via request mocking). Cover: (1) 200 responses for valid amounts, (2) 400 responses for invalid amounts, (3) correct Content-Type headers, (4) X-Pix-Code header present and non-empty, (5) Cache-Control/ETag/Vary headers present, (6) 304 response when If-None-Match matches, (7) /api/pix-result returns HTML fragment with expected elements (img tag, truncated text, copy button). Use Vitest with fetch or Astro's test utilities.
acceptanceCriteria: All test scenarios from PRD Appendix (endpoint tables) pass
Both 200 and 400 response paths are tested
Caching headers are verified on 200 responses
304 response is verified with matching ETag
HTML fragment from /api/pix-result contains expected elements
Tests run via `bun test` or `vitest run`

tasks.title: E2E smoke test for donation flow
tasks.description: Write a Playwright E2E test that exercises the full donation flow on /contributing: (1) navigate to /contributing, (2) verify the donation section is visible after the hero, (3) click the R$ 25 button, (4) verify the QR code image loads and is visible, (5) verify the Copia e Cola section appears with truncated preview, (6) click 'Copiar' and verify clipboard content, (7) type 10000.01 and verify the 'contact us' message appears instead of QR. This serves as a single smoke test proving the critical path works end-to-end.
tasks.userStory: As a visitor, I want to select a donation amount and receive a PIX QR code + Copia e Cola.
acceptanceCriteria: Test navigates to /contributing and finds donation section after hero
Clicking R$ 25 button results in visible QR code image
Copia e Cola truncated preview is visible
Copy button click populates clipboard with PIX string
Entering 10000.01 shows contact us message, no QR
Test passes with `bun run e2e`
